ocr.text: In the analysis of the experimental factors, Table 1 presents five rows with corresponding values for each factor: for Casein, the
degrees of freedom (df) is 1, the estimate is −0.30, the standard error is 0.04, the χ2 value is 66.31, the p-value is < 0.001, and
the hazard ratio is 0.739 with a significance level of ***; for Quercetin, df is 1, the estimate is −0.20, the standard error is 0.04,
the χ2 value is 27.93, the p-value is < 0.001, and the hazard ratio is 0.823, also with significance level of ***; for p-Coumaric
acid, df is 1, the estimate is −0.09, the standard error is 0.04, the χ2 value is 5.93, the p-value is 0.015, and the hazard ratio is
0.914 with a significance level of *; for β-cyfluthrin, df is 1, the estimate is 0.30, the standard error is 0.05, the χ2 value is
42.16, the p-value is < 0.001, and the hazard ratio is 1.345 with significance level of ***; lastly, for Bifenthrin, df is 1, the
estimate is 2.22, the standard error is 0.05, the χ2 value is 1741.64, the p-value is < 0.001, and the hazard ratio is 9.171 with
significance level of ***, indicating a strong effect associated with Bifenthrin.
Experimental factor  df  Estimate  Standard Error  χ2  p  Hazard Ratio
Different amount of alcohol consumption (%)
All the subjects  ≥ 40 g/day  ≥ 60 g/day
Sensitivity (95% CI)  13.5  26.8  46.3
Sensitivity (95% CI)  99.9  99.9  99.8
Turning to Table 2, which categorizes subjects based on different amounts of alcohol consumption, the first row includes the
header "Sensitivity (95% CI)" with values of 13.5 for All the subjects, 26.8 for ≥ 40 g/day, and 46.3 for ≥ 60 g/day, followed by
the confidence intervals represented as 8.3-20.2 for All the subjects, 16.9-38.6 for ≥ 40 g/day, and 30.7-62.6 for ≥ 60 g/day. The
second row again lists "Sensitivity (95% CI)" with values of 99.9 for All the subjects, 99.9 for ≥ 40 g/day, and 99.8 for ≥ 60
g/day, with the corresponding confidence intervals being 99.1-100 for All the subjects, 99.1-100 for ≥ 40 g/day, and 99.1-100
for ≥ 60 g/day. This detailed representation underscores the statistical relevance and variations in sensitivity across different
factors and alcohol consumption levels, highlighting significant findings in both experimental factors and alcohol-related health
outcomes.
